communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Shannon McMinimee (Tacoma Public Schools)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All public records relating to the hiring, employment, contract terms, performance, complaints, concerns, allegations, investigations, affiliations, and separation/termination of Shannon McMinimee, including but not limited to:

1. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding the recruitment, selection, and hiring process for the position filled by Shannon McMinimee, including records regarding any other applicants or candidates who were considered or interviewed for the same position.

2. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her interview process, offer of employment, contract negotiations, onboarding, and appointment.

3. All documents relating to any background check, investigation, or vetting process conducted before or after her hiring, including but not limited to:
a. Communications or records showing what information was sought or obtained about her prior employment history, reasons for separation from any prior employers, or any disciplinary or termination actions at previous workplaces;
b. Any information regarding litigation, claims, settlements, or lawsuits filed by or against Shannon McMinimee and any payouts received;
c. Any documents, emails, or communications about her affiliations or membership in any organizations, including the John Brown Gun Club or other groups, and whether those affiliations were reviewed or disclosed;
d. Identification of who conducted or oversaw any such investigation or vetting process, and any correspondence about their findings or recommendations.

4. Copies of her employment contract(s), amendments, addenda, resignation letter(s), termination notices, settlement or separation agreements, and any related Board resolutions or votes.

5. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her job duties, performance evaluations, contract extensions or amendments.

6. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding any complaints, concerns, grievances, allegations, or reports — whether formal or informal, whether or not investigated — involving Shannon McMinimee, and any related actions, follow-up, or resolution.

7. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her resignation, non-renewal, termination, separation, or any other departure-related discussions.

8. Any communications between Board Members, the Superintendent, HR staff, or legal counsel discussing her recruitment, hiring, background, prior employment, affiliations, litigation history, job performance, contract terms, complaints, concerns, allegations, investigations, or termination.

9. Any records showing payments, settlements, severance, or other financial arrangements related to her employment or separation.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a privilege/exemption log as required by RCW 42.56.210(3), identifying the specific exemption and the basis for it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Public Interest Mainstreaming Parenting

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P000166-040826
communications.communication:
Dear Public Interest Mainstreaming Parenting: 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of Tacoma Public Schools. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 4/8/2026 and given the reference number P000166-040826 for tracking purposes. 
Not all public records are available for release in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available to provide electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the records you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request in "My Request Center".  Your request may be provided in installments. You'll receive an email when your requested records or installment is available. When all records have been provided, you will receive an email the request has been fulfilled and is closed. 
 
Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Tacoma Public Schools

title: Nebraska Public Records Request - State Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Nebraska Public Records Act Request: Nebraska Public Records Request - State Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nebraska Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to Nebraska state government use and governance of generative AI tools, including tools discussed under NITC AI policy section 8-609 such as ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, and related products.

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Adobe AI Assistant, and any other generative AI chatbot tools used by Nebraska state employees for the period from November 8, 2024 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

communications.subject: RE: Nebraska Public Records Act Request: Nebraska Public Records Request - State Generative AI Records
communications.communication: Apologies -- there is a slight miscommunication in this message. I will reach out again with an updated request.
